title: quercus mongolica var. mongolica
intro: Quercus mongolica var. mongolica, also known as the Mongolian oak, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, specifically China and Korea, characterized by its broad, rounded crown and dark green leaves with pointed lobes. The bark of the Mongolian oak has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The tree's leaves and bark are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, providing various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and immune function. Quercus mongolica var. mongolica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine as a powder or capsule.
